use std::cell::Cell;
use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicI32, Ordering};

struct SafeCell<T> {
    value: Cell<T>,
}

impl<T: Copy> SafeCell<T> {
    fn get(&self) -> T {
        self.value.get()
    }

    fn set(&self, val: T) {
        self.value.set(val);
    }
}

fn safe_shared_state() {
    let cell = SafeCell { value: Cell::new(42i32) };
    cell.set(100);
    let val = cell.get();
    println!("{}", val);
}

fn safe_atomic() {
    let atomic = AtomicI32::new(42);
    atomic.store(100, Ordering::Relaxed);
    let val = atomic.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
    println!("{}", val);
}

fn safe_slice_swap(data: &mut [u8], idx1: usize, idx2: usize) {
    data.swap(idx1, idx2);
}
